Dana Levy is a Senior Security Engineer with 5 years of experience in offensive security, threat hunting, and zero-trust architecture design. She holds a Master of Science in Computer Science (Cybersecurity and Cryptography) from the Technion-Israel Institute of Technology (2018–2020). Her expertise spans cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP), identity protection (CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault), and zero-trust frameworks. Notable achievements include designing a zero-trust segmentation system that reduced lateral movement risks by 60% and developing an open-source cloud security misconfiguration scanner with over 200 GitHub stars. She has also led red team automation projects and published research on ML evasion techniques against network intrusion detection systems.
ground_truth: The thesis was on Adversarial Machine Learning Attacks on Network Intrusion Detection Systems — developing novel evasion techniques against ML-based IDS using generative adversarial networks. It received a grade of 97/100 and was published in IEEE S&P Workshop.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: ##Automated Threat Intelligence Platform (CyberArk)
Built an end-to-end threat intelligence aggregation platform ingesting 500K+ indicators of compromise daily from OSINT feeds, commercial threat intel, and internal honeypots. Automated enrichment and correlation with Splunk SIEM. Technologies: Python, Apache Kafka, Elasticsearch, Splunk, Docker.

Section: general
Dana Levy ? Curriculum Vitae

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: ##Open-Source: CloudSec Scanner
Created an open-source cloud security misconfiguration scanner for AWS and Azure. Checks IAM policies, S3 bucket permissions, network ACLs, and encryption settings. 200+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Python, Boto3, Azure SDK.

Section: #Projects
##Zero-Trust Network Architecture (CyberArk)

Designed and implemented a zero-trust segmentation framework for enterprise identity platforms. The system enforces least-privilege access across 50+ microservices using mutual TLS and SPIFFE/SPIRE identity. Reduced lateral movement attack surface by 60%. Technologies: Go, Envoy Proxy, SPIFFE, Kubernetes, Terraform.

Section: #Interests
Bug bounty hunting (HackerOne top 100 in Israel)

Section: #Overview
This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

Section: #Performance
The framework adds less than 2ms latency per request for mTLS handshake and policy evaluation. Tested with 10,000 concurrent connections across 50 microservices.

I am writing to recommend Dana Levy, who has been a key member of my security research team at CyberArk for the past four years. Dana joined us as a security engineer and quickly distinguished herself through exceptional technical depth and leadership ability.

Dana's most impactful contribution has been the design and implementation of our zero-trust network architecture framework. She led a team of 4 engineers to build a SPIFFE/SPIRE-based identity system that reduced our lateral movement attack surface by 60%. This project demonstrated her ability to combine deep security knowledge with practical engineering excellence.

Her work on the automated threat intelligence platform has been equally impressive. Dana architected a system that ingests over 500,000 indicators of compromise daily, dramatically improving our threat detection capabilities. She also built our internal red team automation framework, which has been used in 20+ enterprise client engagements.

Section: #Overview
This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Dana's most impactful contribution has been the design and implementation of our zero-trust network architecture framework. She led a team of 4 engineers to build a SPIFFE/SPIRE-based identity system that reduced our lateral movement attack surface by 60%. This project demonstrated her ability to combine deep security knowledge with practical engineering excellence.

Her work on the automated threat intelligence platform has been equally impressive. Dana architected a system that ingests over 500,000 indicators of compromise daily, dramatically improving our threat detection capabilities. She also built our internal red team automation framework, which has been used in 20+ enterprise client engagements.

Passionate about proactive defense and open-source security research.

Sincerely,

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: #Overview
This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

Section: #Projects
##Zero-Trust Network Architecture (CyberArk)

Designed and implemented a zero-trust segmentation framework for enterprise identity platforms. The system enforces least-privilege access across 50+ microservices using mutual TLS and SPIFFE/SPIRE identity. Reduced lateral movement attack surface by 60%. Technologies: Go, Envoy Proxy, SPIFFE, Kubernetes, Terraform.

2018 ? 2020 | GPA: 92/100

Specialization: Cybersecurity and Cryptography.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: #Performance
The framework adds less than 2ms latency per request for mTLS handshake and policy evaluation. Tested with 10,000 concurrent connections across 50 microservices.

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: DevOps Engineer — Wix.com
August 2019 – March 2021 | Tel Aviv, Israel

Managed AWS infrastructure for Wix's editor platform serving millions of users.

Built automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform and Ansible for 100+ microservices.

Developed custom Jenkins pipelines for continuous integration across 50+ repositories.

Implemented centralized logging with ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) processing 5TB+ logs daily.

Reduced infrastructure provisioning time from 2 days to 15 minutes through automation.

Collaborated with security team to implement automated vulnerability scanning in CI pipelines.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Academic: Auto-Scaling Prediction System
Capstone project building an ML-based Kubernetes autoscaler that predicts traffic patterns using LSTM networks and pre-scales pods accordingly. Reduced over-provisioning by 30% in test environments. Technologies: Python, TensorFlow, Kubernetes, Prometheus.

Section: Kubernetes Custom Autoscaler (Monday.com)
Developed a custom Kubernetes autoscaler using predictive scaling based on historical traffic patterns. Combines HPA metrics with ML predictions to pre-scale pods before traffic spikes. Reduced over-provisioning costs by 30%. Technologies: Go, Python, Prometheus, Kubernetes CRDs.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

- **HAProxy**: Employed for high-availability traffic management.  
- **Cloudflare**: Leveraged for DNS and DDoS protection.  
- **Cert-Manager**: Automated TLS certificate management for Kubernetes clusters.  
- **Terraform**: Used to provision secure network infrastructure (e.g., AWS VPCs, security groups).  

### **Security Tools & Practices**  
- **HashiCorp Vault**: Integrated for secrets management and secure credential storage.  
- **Automated Vulnerability Scanning**: Implemented in CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Jenkins, GitHub Actions) to detect security issues.  
- **Security Policies**: Enforced via open-source tools like **TerraLint**, which validates Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) for compliance and security best practices.  
- **Observability Tools**: Used **Datadog** and **Grafana** to monitor security events and system health.  

### **Key Experience**  
- At **Wix.com**, collaborated with the security team to embed vulnerability scanning into CI/CD workflows.  
- At **Monday.com**, designed a self-service deployment platform with security-focused features like feature flags and deployment health checks.  

These tools and practices reflect a strong focus on secure, scalable infrastructure and DevOps security hygiene.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Contributing
Contributions welcome. See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines. Please include tests for all new features.

question: Tell me about the multi-cloud migration project.
answer:

- **Framework Development**: Created an automated migration framework with features like service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting (using Istio), and rollback capabilities, enabling zero-downtime migrations.

- **Observability**: Enhanced monitoring with tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog across 500+ services.

The project was highlighted in a letter of recommendation as a "transformative contribution," showcasing the candidate's expertise in large-scale infrastructure transitions. Technologies used included Terraform, Kubernetes, Istio, Go, and GitOps (ArgoCD).

This project was highlighted as a transformative contribution in internal recommendations and is tied to the candidate's role as a Senior DevOps Engineer at Monday.com.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Multi-Cloud Migration Framework (Monday.com)
Designed a framework for migrating 30+ services from single-cloud AWS to hybrid AWS/GCP architecture. Includes automated service dependency mapping, traffic shifting with Istio, and rollback capabilities. Reduced cloud costs by 25% through workload optimization. Technologies: Terraform, Kubernetes, Istio, Go.

Section: Key Features
Automated service dependency discovery and mapping

Progressive traffic shifting with configurable canary percentages

Real-time health monitoring with automatic rollback on degradation

Multi-cloud cost optimization and workload placement

Terraform state migration between cloud providers

25% cloud cost reduction through workload optimization

Capstone project building an ML-based Kubernetes autoscaler that predicts traffic patterns using LSTM networks and pre-scales pods accordingly. Reduced over-provisioning by 30% in test environments. Technologies: Python, TensorFlow, Kubernetes, Prometheus.

question: What technologies were used in the autoscaler project?
answer:
The autoscaler project utilized the following technologies:

**Core Technologies:**
- **Programming Languages:** Go, Python (for predictive scaling logic and ML models)
- **Cloud Platforms:** AWS (EKS, EC2, S3, RDS), GCP (GKE, Cloud SQL)
- **Container Orchestration:** Kubernetes (with CRDs for custom resource definitions)
- **Monitoring/observability:** Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog
- **Machine Learning:** TensorFlow (for LSTM-based traffic prediction in the academic project)

**Additional Tools:**
- **Infrastructure as Code:** Terraform, Helm, Kustomize
- **Service Mesh:** Istio (for traffic management in production systems)
- **CI/CD:** ArgoCD, GitHub Actions

These tools enabled predictive scaling, multi-cloud orchestration, and observability for the autoscaler projects.

1. **Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)**  
   - Enabled 150+ developers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently.  
   - Features: Automated canary releases, feature flags, deployment health checks.  
   - Reduced deployment lead time from **4 hours to 15 minutes**.  
   - Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.  

2. **Multi-Cloud Migration Framework (Monday.com)**  
   - Facilitated migration of 30+ services from AWS to a hybrid AWS/GCP architecture.  
   - Reduced cloud costs by **25%**.  
   - Enabled automated service dependency mapping and progressive traffic shifting.  

3. **Observability Stack (Monday.com)**  
   - Built a comprehensive monitoring system using Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog.  
   - Provided visibility into **500+ services** across 15 Kubernetes clusters.  

4. **Auto-Scaling Prediction System (Capstone Project, BGU)**  
   - Developed an ML-based Kubernetes autoscaler using LSTM networks.  
   - Reduced over-provisioning by **30%** in test environments.  
   - Technologies: Python, TensorFlow, Kubernetes, Prometheus.  

5. **Infrastructure Automation Tools (Wix.com)**  
   - Created Terraform/Ansible workflows for provisioning 100+ microservices.  
   - Reduced infrastructure provisioning time from **2 days to 15 minutes**.  
   - Implemented centralized logging with the ELK stack (5TB+ logs/day).  

These systems highlight expertise in cloud infrastructure, automation, and scalable platform design.

Section: DevOps Engineer — Wix.com
August 2019 – March 2021 | Tel Aviv, Israel

Managed AWS infrastructure for Wix's editor platform serving millions of users.

Built automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform and Ansible for 100+ microservices.

Developed custom Jenkins pipelines for continuous integration across 50+ repositories.

Implemented centralized logging with ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) processing 5TB+ logs daily.

Reduced infrastructure provisioning time from 2 days to 15 minutes through automation.

Collaborated with security team to implement automated vulnerability scanning in CI pipelines.

Section: Senior DevOps Engineer — Monday.com
April 2021 – Present | Tel Aviv, Israel

Architecting multi-cloud infrastructure (AWS + GCP) serving 200K+ daily active users.

Leading a platform engineering team of 5, establishing SRE practices and on-call rotations.

Designed and implemented a multi-cloud migration framework moving 30+ services from AWS to a hybrid AWS/GCP architecture, reducing cloud costs by 25%.

Built self-service deployment platform enabling 150+ developers to deploy independently with automated canary releases.

Managed 15 Kubernetes clusters (EKS and GKE) with 2,000+ pods, implementing custom autoscaling policies.

Reduced CI/CD pipeline execution time by 60% through parallelization, caching, and build optimization.

Implemented GitOps workflow using ArgoCD, reducing deployment errors by 40%.

Built comprehensive observability stack with Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog covering 500+ services.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Yoni Abergel
His self-service deployment platform has been equally impactful, enabling 150+ developers to deploy independently and reducing deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Yoni also built our observability stack from the ground up, giving our teams unprecedented visibility into system health across 500+ services.

Yoni is an exceptional team leader. He built our platform engineering team from scratch, established SRE practices, and created a culture of automation and reliability. His technical blog posts are widely read within the company and have helped onboard dozens of engineers.

I wholeheartedly recommend Yoni for a Staff DevOps or Platform Engineering role. He combines deep infrastructure expertise with strategic thinking and strong leadership abilities.

Sincerely,

Nir Zohar

VP of Engineering

Monday.com

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Kubernetes Custom Autoscaler (Monday.com)
Developed a custom Kubernetes autoscaler using predictive scaling based on historical traffic patterns. Combines HPA metrics with ML predictions to pre-scale pods before traffic spikes. Reduced over-provisioning costs by 30%. Technologies: Go, Python, Prometheus, Kubernetes CRDs.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Designed a framework for migrating 30+ services from single-cloud AWS to hybrid AWS/GCP architecture. Technologies: Terraform, Kubernetes, Istio, Go.

Language: Go, Python

Service Mesh: Istio for traffic management

Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog

CI/CD: ArgoCD, GitHub Actions

Cloud: AWS (EKS, S3, RDS), GCP (GKE, Cloud SQL)

Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Yoni Abergel
From: Nir Zohar, VP of Engineering at Monday.com

Date: May 20, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am pleased to recommend Yoni Abergel, who has been a cornerstone of our platform engineering organization at Monday.com for over five years. Yoni joined as a DevOps engineer and rapidly grew into a senior technical leader.

Yoni's most transformative contribution was designing and executing our multi-cloud migration framework. He led the migration of 30+ services from a single-cloud AWS setup to a hybrid AWS/GCP architecture, achieving a 25% reduction in cloud costs while maintaining zero downtime. This project showcased his exceptional ability to plan and execute complex infrastructure changes at scale.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Yoni Abergel
His self-service deployment platform has been equally impactful, enabling 150+ developers to deploy independently and reducing deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Yoni also built our observability stack from the ground up, giving our teams unprecedented visibility into system health across 500+ services.

Yoni is an exceptional team leader. He built our platform engineering team from scratch, established SRE practices, and created a culture of automation and reliability. His technical blog posts are widely read within the company and have helped onboard dozens of engineers.

I wholeheartedly recommend Yoni for a Staff DevOps or Platform Engineering role. He combines deep infrastructure expertise with strategic thinking and strong leadership abilities.

Sincerely,

Nir Zohar

VP of Engineering

Monday.com

nir.zohar@monday.com

2. **Remote Workability**:  
   - Based in **Tel Aviv, Israel**, which is geographically adjacent to Europe and often included in remote work regions. Time zones may require flexibility (e.g., overlapping hours with Central/Eastern Europe).  

3. **Availability**:

question: What unique combination of skills does this candidate offer?
answer:
The candidate offers a unique blend of **multi-cloud expertise**, **DevOps automation**, and **complex system architecture** skills. Here's the breakdown:

### **Core Technical Skills**
- **Cloud Mastery**: AWS (EKS, EC2, Lambda, RDS) and GCP (GKE, Cloud Run, BigQuery) proficiency, with hands-on experience in hybrid architectures.
- **Infrastructure as Code (IaC)**: Advanced Terraform expertise (led migrations) and Kubernetes orchestration (EKS/GKE clusters).
- **Observability Stack**: Comprehensive setup using Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, and ELK for 500+ services.
- **CI/CD Automation**: Built self-service deployment platforms with ArgoCD, GitHub Actions, and Jenkins, reducing pipeline time by 60%.

### **Unique Projects & Frameworks**
- **Multi-Cloud Migration Framework**: Designed a zero-downtime framework to migrate 30+ services from AWS to hybrid AWS/GCP, cutting cloud costs by 25% via workload optimization.
- **Progressive Traffic Shifting**: Implemented canary release strategies with Istio for gradual service transitions.
- **GitOps & SRE Practices**: Established GitOps workflows and on-call rotations for 150+ developers, reducing deployment errors by 40%.

### **Differentiators**
- **Leadership**: Led teams of 5+ in large-scale infrastructure projects (e.g., 15 Kubernetes clusters for 2K+ pods).
- **Security & Compliance**: Integrated automated vulnerability scanning in CI pipelines and managed HashiCorp Vault for secrets management.
- **Cost Optimization**: Expertise in multi-cloud cost modeling and resource allocation.

This combination of **cloud-native expertise**, **automation leadership**, and **complex system design** positions the candidate as a rare DevOps/SRE generalist.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Maya Chen
From: Lior Barak, Frontend Team Lead at Fiverr Date: May 25, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am delighted to recommend Maya Chen, who has been an exceptional member of our frontend engineering team at Fiverr for the past three years. Maya joined us straight from university and has rapidly become one of our most impactful engineers.

Maya's standout achievement has been leading our design system initiative. She built and maintains a component library used by 80+ engineers, with 60+ accessible components that have reduced UI development time by 40%. Her deep knowledge of accessibility standards led to our marketplace achieving full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across 20+ product surfaces.

Her work on web performance has been equally impressive. Maya improved our Largest Contentful Paint by 40% and reduced JavaScript bundle size by 35%, directly impacting user experience for 4M+ monthly active users. She also built our real-time collaborative editor using cutting-edge CRDT algorithms.

Section: #Contributing
Follow the contribution guide in CONTRIBUTING.md. All components must include accessibility tests, Storybook stories, and TypeScript types.

Section: #Architecture
The system uses a monorepo architecture with Turborepo, organizing components into three tiers: Primitives (atoms like Button, Input, Typography), Composites (molecules like SearchBar, Card, Modal), and Patterns (organisms like Navigation, DataTable, FormWizard). Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

Section: #Overview
The Fiverr Design System is a shared component library used by 80+ frontend engineers across 20+ product surfaces. Built with React, TypeScript, and Storybook, it provides 60+ accessible components with automated visual regression testing and comprehensive documentation.

Section: #Overview
The Fiverr Design System is a shared component library used by 80+ frontend engineers across 20+ product surfaces. Built with React, TypeScript, and Storybook, it provides 60+ accessible components with automated visual regression testing and comprehensive documentation.

Section: #Architecture
The system uses a monorepo architecture with Turborepo, organizing components into three tiers: Primitives (atoms like Button, Input, Typography), Composites (molecules like SearchBar, Card, Modal), and Patterns (organisms like Navigation, DataTable, FormWizard). Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

Section: #Contributing
Follow the contribution guide in CONTRIBUTING.md. All components must include accessibility tests, Storybook stories, and TypeScript types.

Section: #Overview
The Fiverr Design System is a shared component library used by 80+ frontend engineers across 20+ product surfaces. Built with React, TypeScript, and Storybook, it provides 60+ accessible components with automated visual regression testing and comprehensive documentation.

Section: #Architecture
The system uses a monorepo architecture with Turborepo, organizing components into three tiers: Primitives (atoms like Button, Input, Typography), Composites (molecules like SearchBar, Card, Modal), and Patterns (organisms like Navigation, DataTable, FormWizard). Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

This project underscores her expertise in design systems, accessibility, and scalable frontend architecture.

This feature directly supported Fiverr’s marketplace by improving collaboration efficiency among sellers.

question: What technologies were used in the performance optimization project?
answer:
The performance optimization project utilized the following technologies and techniques:

**Key Technologies:**
- **Core Web Vitals Monitoring:** Automated tracking of metrics like LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) and CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift).
- **Bundle Analysis Tools:** Tools like Webpack for analyzing JavaScript bundle sizes and optimizing dependencies.
- **CI/CD Integration:** GitHub Actions for automating performance regression detection in continuous integration pipelines.
- **AWS Services:** S3 for storage, CloudFront for CDN (Content Delivery Network) to accelerate asset delivery, and Lambda for serverless compute optimizations.
- **Performance Optimization Techniques:** Code splitting, lazy loading, image optimization, tree shaking, and dynamic imports to reduce bundle sizes and improve load times.

**Results:** Achieved a 40% improvement in LCP and a 35% reduction in JavaScript bundle size, directly enhancing user experience for 4M+ monthly active users.

question: What systems has the candidate built?
answer:
The candidate has built the following systems and tools:  

1. **Fiverr Design System**  
   - A shared component library used by 80+ frontend engineers.  
   - Built with **React, TypeScript, and Storybook**, featuring 60+ accessible components, automated visual regression testing (Chromatic), and comprehensive documentation.  

2. **Real-time Collaborative Editor**  
   - A collaborative editing feature for gig descriptions using **WebSockets, CRDT algorithms (Yjs), React, Node.js**, and conflict resolution for concurrent edits.  

3. **Web Performance Optimization Suite**  
   - An internal toolkit for monitoring and optimizing performance, including automated Core Web Vitals tracking, bundle analysis, and CI/CD integration.  
   - Improved **LCP by 40%** and reduced JavaScript bundle size by **35%**.  

4. **AccessiCheck (Academic Capstone)**  
   - An AI-powered accessibility audit tool using **Python, React, Playwright, and GPT API** to generate WCAG 2.1 compliance reports with auto-fix suggestions.  

5. **ReactA11y Hooks (Open-Source)**  
   - A library of React hooks for accessibility patterns (focus management, screen reader announcements), with **300+ GitHub stars**.  

These systems highlight expertise in React, TypeScript, accessibility, and performance optimization.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Conversational AI Customer Support Bot (DataSphere Technologies)
Developed an NLP chatbot using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture. The bot integrates with the company's internal knowledge base to provide accurate, contextual responses. Successfully handles 70% of inbound support queries without human intervention. Technologies: LangChain, Hugging Face Transformers, ChromaDB, FastAPI.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Section: Contributing
Contributions welcome. Please follow the ML engineering guidelines in CONTRIBUTING.md. All model changes require offline evaluation before deployment.

Section: Performance
Serving 2M+ users with sub-50ms p99 latency. Model training completes in 4 hours on 4x V100 GPUs. Daily retraining pipeline runs automatically via Airflow with drift detection.

Section: Key Features
Hybrid collaborative + content-based recommendation

Real-time inference with sub-50ms latency via Redis caching

A/B testing framework for model comparison

Distributed training on AWS SageMaker

Automatic model retraining on data drift detection

35% improvement in click-through rate over rule-based baseline

Uses an ensemble of gradient boosting and neural network models. Technologies: Apache Kafka, PyTorch, Docker, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

2017 – 2021 | GPA: 89/100

Capstone Project: Autonomous Drone Navigation — built a computer vision system for indoor drone navigation using reinforcement learning with visual inputs (PyTorch, OpenCV, ROS).

Dean's List: 2019, 2020.

Teaching Assistant for Introduction to Machine Learning (2020-2021).

Professional Scrum Master I — PSM I (June 2022, Scrum.org)

Section: Overview
The Smart Recommendation Engine combines matrix factorization with deep neural networks for content understanding to deliver personalized recommendations at scale. Built for DataSphere Technologies' e-commerce platform, it improved click-through rate by 35% compared to the previous rule-based system.

Section: Open-Source Contribution: LangChain
Contributed a document loader module for Hebrew text processing to the LangChain open-source project. The contribution was merged and received 50+ stars on GitHub. Included tokenization support for Hebrew morphology and right-to-left text handling.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
Equally impressive is his work on the real-time fraud detection pipeline, which processes 10,000 transactions per second with sub-100ms inference latency. Ofir architected the entire streaming ML pipeline using Kafka and PyTorch, showing his ability to deliver under strict performance constraints.

As a team leader, Ofir has been instrumental in growing our ML team. He leads 3 engineers with a focus on code quality, mentoring, and knowledge sharing. He conducts thorough code reviews, runs sprint planning, and has created onboarding materials that significantly reduced ramp-up time for new hires. His contribution to LangChain's open-source project (Hebrew text processing) reflects his commitment to the broader community.

I recommend Ofir without hesitation for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer role. He is a rare engineer who combines deep ML expertise with production engineering skills and strong leadership abilities.

Sincerely,

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

question: Tell me about the recommendation engine project.
answer:
The **Smart Recommendation Engine** project, developed by Ofir Ohan at DataSphere Technologies, is a hybrid system combining **collaborative filtering** and **content-based recommendations** to deliver personalized suggestions at scale. Key highlights include:

- **Impact**: Achieved a **35% increase in click-through rates** compared to the previous rule-based system, serving over **2 million users**.
- **Architecture**: Integrates **matrix factorization** (for collaborative filtering) with **deep neural networks** (for content understanding), enabling real-time inference with **sub-50ms latency** via **Redis caching**.
- **Scalability**: Built on **AWS SageMaker**, handling massive datasets (over 50 million rows) and supporting distributed training.
- **Technologies**: PyTorch, FastAPI, Redis, AWS SageMaker, and ChromaDB (for RAG-based features in related projects).
- **Additional Contributions**: Ofir also designed a **conversational AI chatbot** (using LangChain and Hugging Face Transformers) and a **real-time fraud detection pipeline** (with Apache Kafka and PyTorch).

This project exemplifies his expertise in bridging **research and production** for high-impact ML systems.

Architected a real-time fraud detection pipeline processing 10,000 transactions per second with sub-100ms latency using Apache Kafka, PyTorch, and Kubernetes.

Reduced model inference latency by 40% through TensorRT optimization and model distillation techniques.

Worked extensively with large-scale datasets exceeding 50 million rows on AWS infrastructure (SageMaker, EC2, S3, Lambda).

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

question: What technologies were used in the chatbot project?
answer:
The chatbot project utilized the following technologies:  
- **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture**  
- **LangChain** for orchestrating the AI workflow  
- **Hugging Face Transformers** for NLP model integration  
- **ChromaDB** for vector storage and retrieval  
- **FastAPI** for building the backend API  
- **AWS SageMaker** and **EC2** for training and hosting  
- **Redis** for caching  
- **PyTorch** for model training (mentioned in the Tech Stack section).  

These tools enabled the chatbot to handle 70% of inbound queries autonomously.

Built an end-to-end streaming ML pipeline for transaction fraud detection in a fintech environment. The system processes 10,000 transactions per second with sub-100ms inference latency. Section: Technical Skills
Programming Languages: Python, SQL, JavaScript, Bash

The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Tech Stack
Deep Learning: PyTorch for model training and inference

Serving: FastAPI with Redis caching

Training Infrastructure: AWS SageMaker for distributed training

Data Pipeline: Apache Spark for feature engineering, Apache Airflow for orchestration

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Section: Tech Stack
Deep Learning: PyTorch for model training and inference

Serving: FastAPI with Redis caching

Training Infrastructure: AWS SageMaker for distributed training

Feature Store: Redis for real-time features, S3 for batch features

Monitoring: Weights & Biases for experiment tracking, Prometheus for serving metrics

Data Pipeline: Apache Spark for feature engineering, Apache Airflow for orchestration

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Usage
The system exposes a REST API for real-time recommendations. Configure user and item feature sources in config.yaml. Training pipelines run on SageMaker with configurable hyperparameters.

Section: Key Features
Hybrid collaborative + content-based recommendation

Real-time inference with sub-50ms latency via Redis caching

A/B testing framework for model comparison

Distributed training on AWS SageMaker

Automatic model retraining on data drift detection

35% improvement in click-through rate over rule-based baseline

Section: Smart Recommendation Engine (DataSphere Technologies)
Built a hybrid collaborative and content-based recommendation system serving 2M+ users. The system combines matrix factorization with deep neural networks for content understanding. Improved click-through rate by 35% compared to the previous rule-based system. Technologies: PyTorch, Redis, FastAPI, AWS SageMaker.

Section: Summary
Machine Learning Engineer with 4 years of experience designing and deploying production ML systems. Specialized in NLP, recommendation systems, and real-time data pipelines. Proven track record of leading engineering teams and delivering high-impact solutions in fintech and e-commerce domains. Passionate about bridging the gap between research and production-ready AI.

Section: Overview
The Smart Recommendation Engine combines matrix factorization with deep neural networks for content understanding to deliver personalized recommendations at scale. Built for DataSphere Technologies' e-commerce platform, it improved click-through rate by 35% compared to the previous rule-based system.

Section: Certifications
AWS Certified Machine Learning — Specialty (March 2023, Amazon Web Services)

Deep Learning Specialization (September 2022, DeepLearning.AI via Coursera)

Professional Scrum Master I — PSM I (June 2022, Scrum.org)

Developed an NLP chatbot using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture. The bot integrates with the company's internal knowledge base to provide accurate, contextual responses. Successfully handles 70% of inbound support queries without human intervention. Technologies: LangChain, Hugging Face Transformers, ChromaDB, FastAPI.

Section: Performance
Serving 2M+ users with sub-50ms p99 latency. Model training completes in 4 hours on 4x V100 GPUs. Daily retraining pipeline runs automatically via Airflow with drift detection.

Section: Real-time Fraud Detection Pipeline (DataSphere Technologies)
Built an end-to-end streaming ML pipeline for transaction fraud detection in a fintech environment. The system processes 10,000 transactions per second with sub-100ms inference latency. Uses an ensemble of gradient boosting and neural network models. Technologies: Apache Kafka, PyTorch, Docker, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Interests
Contributing to open-source ML projects

Reading research papers on transformer architectures and efficient inference

Competitive programming (LeetCode top 5%)

Hiking and photography

Contributed a document loader module for Hebrew text processing to the LangChain open-source project. The contribution was merged and received 50+ stars on GitHub. Included tokenization support for Hebrew morphology and right-to-left text handling.

Contributions welcome. Please follow the ML engineering guidelines in CONTRIBUTING.md. All model changes require offline evaluation before deployment.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Contributed a document loader module for Hebrew text processing to the LangChain open-source project. The contribution was merged and received 50+ stars on GitHub. Included tokenization support for Hebrew morphology and right-to-left text handling.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

- Backend: Node js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: ##Real-Time Publisher Analytics Dashboard (Unity)
Designed and shipped a real-time analytics platform for 50K+ game developers to monitor ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. Features include custom segmentation, anomaly detection alerts, and revenue forecasting

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

- Backend: Node js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Key Features
- Real-time ad revenue monitoring with 30-second refresh

- Custom segmentation by country, ad format, app version, and user cohort

- ML-powered anomaly detection with Slack/email alerts

- Revenue forecasting with confidence intervals

- Exportable reports in CSV and PDF formats

- Used by 50K+ game developers worldwide

question: Tell me about the ad mediation project.
answer:
The ad mediation project involved leading the development of an **ML-powered ad mediation algorithm** at Unity Technologies, which optimized waterfall configurations in real-time. This initiative increased **publisher revenue by 22%** compared to manual management and impacted **1.5B monthly end-users**. Key contributions included:  
- Collaborating with ML teams on feature engineering and model evaluation.  
- Designing a real-time analytics dashboard used by **50K+ game developers** for monetization insights.  
- Reducing SDK integration time for mediation platforms from 5 days to 2 hours.  
- Implementing bidding waterfall optimization, boosting eCPM by 15% for publishers.  

The project scaled to process **10B+ daily ad events** with sub-5-second latency, leveraging a tech stack including **React, Kafka, Flink, BigQuery, and GCP**. It emphasized data-driven decision-making through custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting.

question: What was the outcome of the analytics dashboard project?
answer:
The analytics dashboard project, specifically the **Real-Time Publisher Analytics Dashboard** developed at Unity Technologies, achieved the following outcomes:  

1. **User Impact**:  
   - Deployed globally, it is used by **50K+ game developers** to monitor ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs in real time.  
   - Enabled data-driven decision-making through features like custom segmentation, anomaly detection alerts, and revenue forecasting.  

2. **Technical Performance**:  
   - Processes **10B+ daily ad events** with **sub-5-second latency** for critical metrics and **<2-second page load times**.  
   - Built on a scalable architecture using **Kafka + Flink** for real-time processing, **BigQuery** for historical analytics, and a **React-based frontend** with interactive visualizations.  

3. **Business Impact**:  
   - Enhanced publisher visibility into monetization performance, directly supporting optimization of ad strategies and user acquisition ROI improvements.  
   - Part of Unity’s monetization platform, serving **1.5B monthly end-users** across mobile gaming.  

This project solidified Unity’s capabilities in providing actionable insights for developers while demonstrating scalability and real-time analytics excellence.

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

- Backend: Node js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

- Backend: Node js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

- Backend: Node js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

**1. Proven Leadership & Scalability:**  
- 5+ years of experience leading high-impact product strategies for platforms serving **1.5B monthly end-users** (Unity) and **10K+ developers** (ironSource).

**3. Technical Depth & Collaboration:**

**4. Strategic Vision & Execution:**  
- Translated product vision into scalable solutions, securing **$5M budget** for analytics platforms and presenting to C-suite/executives.  
- Demonstrated ability to balance technical execution (e.g., ML model integration) with business outcomes (e.g., revenue growth).  

**5. People Leadership:**

- Honed customer empathy through **200+ developer interviews**, ensuring product decisions align with user needs.

Their track record of driving innovation and growth aligns with the demands of leading high-stakes, high-impact product initiatives.

contexts: Tal Ben-David is a Staff Backend Engineer with 7 years of professional experience specializing in distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and high-throughput data pipelines. He holds a Master of Science in Computer Science from Tel Aviv University (2018) and has expertise in Kafka, Flink, ClickHouse, Kubernetes, Go, Redis, and AWS. His career highlights include designing a Raft variant optimized for cross-datacenter replication, reducing commit latency by 40% and published at ACM SOSP Workshop. Tal has also led the decomposition of a monolithic Clojure application into 40+ microservices, cutting deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours. Notably, he developed an open-source Kafka consumer framework in Java/Kotlin with 250+ GitHub stars, featuring advanced retry and backpressure mechanisms.

**Tal Ben David** is a Software Engineer with 5+ years of professional experience. He holds a Bachelor’s in Computer Science from Tel Aviv University (2016) and a Master’s in Data Science from the same institution (2018). His expertise lies in building high-throughput streaming pipelines using Java 17, Kotlin, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and ClickHouse, with a focus on real-time data processing and fraud detection. His most notable achievement includes designing a multi-region streaming pipeline that processes 1.2M events/second with sub-100ms latency, handling 100B+ daily events while ensuring zero data loss during failovers.

Tal Ben-David, a Senior Engineer at AppsFlyer with over six years of professional experience, holds an MSc in Computer Science. He specializes in distributed systems, real-time event processing, and microservices architecture, with expertise in exactly-once semantics, stateful processing, and multi-region consistency. His most notable achievement is designing the real-time event enrichment pipeline, processing 1.2M events/second, and leading the microservices migration that reduced deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours. Tal also developed the KafkaFlow open-source project, which has 250+ stars, and mentors senior engineers as the architecture guild leader. His work has significantly enhanced system quality and engineering velocity at AppsFlyer.

- **Role**: Designed distributed systems and event-driven architectures at scale, specializing in high-throughput data pipelines, microservices, and platform reliability.  
- **Key Contributions**:  
  - Built systems processing **100B+ daily events** with **sub-millisecond latency**.  
  - Developed a **Raft variant** for cross-datacenter replication, reducing commit latency by **40%**.  
  - Led real-time event enrichment pipelines for mobile attribution, integrating fraud detection and metadata enrichment.  
- **Tech Stack**: Java 17/Kotlin, Apache Kafka/Flink, ClickHouse/Redis, Kubernetes (EKS), Prometheus, and more.  

No specific details about Amdocs are included in the provided documents. Let me know if you'd like further clarification!

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Tal Ben-David
From: Reshef Mann, CTO at AppsFlyer

Date: May 22, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I recommend Tal Ben-David, who has been a technical cornerstone at AppsFlyer for over six years. Tal joined as a backend engineer and quickly became one of our most influential architects.

Tal's flagship achievement is the real-time event enrichment pipeline processing 1.2M events/second — the backbone of our attribution platform. He designed the architecture from scratch, solving complex challenges around exactly-once semantics, stateful processing, and multi-region consistency.

His leadership of the microservices migration — decomposing our monolith into 40+ services — transformed our engineering velocity, reducing deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours. As architecture guild leader, Tal mentors 8 senior engineers and has established design review processes that elevated our system quality.

Section: ##Real-Time Event Enrichment Pipeline (AppsFlyer)
Built a streaming pipeline processing 1.2M events/second for mobile attribution enrichment. Uses Kafka for ingestion, Flink for stateful processing, and ClickHouse for analytics. Technologies: Java, Kafka, Flink, ClickHouse, Kubernetes.

Designed active-active deployment across 3 AWS regions achieving 99.99% uptime. Includes conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for state synchronization and intelligent routing. Technologies: Go, Kafka, Redis, AWS.

Led decomposition of monolithic Clojure application into 40+ microservices with saga-based transactions, API gateway, and service mesh. Reduced deployment cycle from 2 weeks to 2 hours. Technologies: Kotlin, gRPC, Istio, Kubernetes.

Created an open-source Kafka consumer framework for Java/Kotlin with built-in retry, dead-letter queues, and backpressure handling. 250+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Kotlin, Kafka.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Tal Ben-David
From: Reshef Mann, CTO at AppsFlyer

Date: May 22, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I recommend Tal Ben-David, who has been a technical cornerstone at AppsFlyer for over six years. Tal joined as a backend engineer and quickly became one of our most influential architects.

Tal's flagship achievement is the real-time event enrichment pipeline processing 1.2M events/second — the backbone of our attribution platform. He designed the architecture from scratch, solving complex challenges around exactly-once semantics, stateful processing, and multi-region consistency.

His leadership of the microservices migration — decomposing our monolith into 40+ services — transformed our engineering velocity, reducing deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours. As architecture guild leader, Tal mentors 8 senior engineers and has established design review processes that elevated our system quality.

Section: ##Real-Time Event Enrichment Pipeline (AppsFlyer)
Built a streaming pipeline processing 1.2M events/second for mobile attribution enrichment. Uses Kafka for ingestion, Flink for stateful processing, and ClickHouse for analytics. Technologies: Java, Kafka, Flink, ClickHouse, Kubernetes.

Designed active-active deployment across 3 AWS regions achieving 99.99% uptime. Includes conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for state synchronization and intelligent routing. Technologies: Go, Kafka, Redis, AWS.

Led decomposition of monolithic Clojure application into 40+ microservices with saga-based transactions, API gateway, and service mesh. Reduced deployment cycle from 2 weeks to 2 hours. Technologies: Kotlin, gRPC, Istio, Kubernetes.

Created an open-source Kafka consumer framework for Java/Kotlin with built-in retry, dead-letter queues, and backpressure handling. 250+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Kotlin, Kafka.

Section: ##Real-Time Event Enrichment Pipeline (AppsFlyer)
Designed a Raft variant optimized for cross-datacenter replication reducing commit latency by 40%. Implemented prototype in Go and evaluated across 5 simulated datacenters. Published at ACM SOSP Workshop.

Section: Overview
Processes 1.2M events/second for real-time mobile attribution enrichment at AppsFlyer. Ingests raw attribution events, enriches with device/geo/campaign metadata, applies fraud detection rules, and routes to analytics and reporting systems.

Section: Architecture
Three-stage pipeline: Kafka ingestion layer with 500+ partitions, Flink stateful processing with RocksDB state backend for sessionization and deduplication, and ClickHouse sink for real-time OLAP queries. Multi-region deployment with Kafka MirrorMaker 2 for cross-region replication.

question: What are the candidate's primary technical frameworks and tools?
answer:
The candidate's primary technical frameworks and tools include:

**Languages/Platforms**:  
- **Java 17**, **Kotlin**  
- **Go** (for distributed systems and cross-datacenter replication projects)  

**Streaming & Processing**:

- **Prometheus**, **Grafana**, **Datadog**

- **gRPC**, **Istio** (service mesh), **CRDTs** (conflict-free data types)  
- **Kafka consumer framework** (open-source project in Kotlin/Java)  

These tools reflect their expertise in distributed systems, high-throughput pipelines, and cloud-native architectures.

His expertise lies in building high-throughput streaming pipelines using Java 17, Kotlin, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and ClickHouse, with a focus on real-time data processing and fraud detection. His most notable achievement includes designing a multi-region streaming pipeline that processes 1.2M events/second with sub-100ms latency, handling 100B+ daily events while ensuring zero data loss during failovers.

### **Infrastructure Experience**
- **Cloud Infrastructure**: Designed multi-region AWS deployments with conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for state synchronization.
- **Networking & Routing**: Implemented intelligent routing in active-active architectures.
- **Storage & Orchestration**: Used S3, Redis, and Kubernetes for scalable infrastructure management.

These details confirm strong expertise in both DevOps practices and infrastructure design.

question: What streaming and data processing tools does the candidate know?
answer:
The candidate is proficient with the following **streaming and data processing tools**, as evidenced by their projects and tech stack:

---

### **Streaming Tools**  
- **Apache Kafka**: Used for high-throughput event ingestion (e.g., 1.2M events/second for mobile attribution pipelines).  
- **Apache Flink**: Employed for stateful stream processing, real-time fraud detection, and windowed sessionization.  
- **Kubernetes (EKS)**: Orchestration for scalable, fault-tolerant streaming architectures.  

---

### **Data Processing & Storage Tools**  
- **ClickHouse**: Used for analytics and real-time querying of processed events.  
- **Redis**: Leveraged for caching, state synchronization (CRDTs), and low-latency lookups.  
- **S3**: Integrated for storage of raw/event data.  
- **Protocol Buffers / Avro**: Used for efficient serialization in data pipelines.  

---

### **Additional Relevant Tools**  
- **Terraform**: For infrastructure-as-code deployment of streaming systems.  
- **Prometheus / Grafana**: Monitoring tools for pipeline performance and latency tracking.  

These tools are highlighted in their real-time event enrichment pipeline, microservices architecture, and open-source contributions (e.g., Kafka consumer framework).

question: What was the outcome of the multi-region architecture project?
answer:
The multi-region architecture project achieved **99.99% uptime** through an **active-active deployment** across 3 AWS regions. Key outcomes include:  
- **Zero data loss** during region failovers, enabled by conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) and intelligent routing.  
- **Sub-100ms end-to-end latency** for real-time processing of 1.2M events/second.  
- **Cross-region consistency** via Kafka MirrorMaker 2 for replication and CRDTs for state synchronization.  
- **Zero data loss** during region failovers, ensuring reliability for 100B+ daily events.  

This architecture became the backbone of AppsFlyer’s attribution platform, reducing deployment cycles to 2 hours and setting a benchmark for multi-region system resilience.

question: What technologies were used in the microservices migration?
answer:
The microservices migration utilized the following technologies:  
- **Kotlin** (for service development)  
- **gRPC** (for inter-service communication)  
- **Istio** (service mesh for traffic management)  
- **Kubernetes** (container orchestration)  
- **Java** (legacy monolith and some services)  
- **Kafka** (event streaming and messaging)  
- **Flink** (stateful processing)  
- **Redis** (caching and session state)  
- **AWS** (cloud infrastructure)  
- **ClickHouse** (analytics storage)  
- **Terraform** (infrastructure as code)  
- **Prometheus/Grafana** (monitoring and observability).  

These tools enabled the decomposition of the monolith into 40+ microservices, reducing deployment cycles to 2 hours.

question: What is this project?
answer:
This project is an AI-powered system designed to help recruiters evaluate job candidates more effectively by transforming their uploaded documents (e.g., CVs, certificates) into an interactive, evidence-based digital representative. Here's a summary:

### **Core Purpose**  
Recruiters struggle to discern verified facts from self-promotion in static documents. This system addresses this by:  
- Quantifying a candidate's skill proficiency using a trained machine learning model (1-5 scale).  
- Providing instant, deterministic answers to structured questions (e.g., education, experience) via a JSON-based structured store.  
- Allowing recruiters to ask natural language questions about a candidate’s projects, certifications, or technical expertise, with answers grounded in the candidate’s own documents.  

### **Key Features**  
1. **Dual Deployment Options**:  
   - **Local**: Open-source stack for development/training.  
   - **Cloud**: Low-cost, always-on API service with security measures (e.g., hidden setup pages, private vector databases).  

2. **Data Architecture**:  
   - **Structured Store**: JSON file holding verified profile fields and precomputed skill scores.  
   - **Vector Store**: ChromaDB for semantic search, isolating candidate data from project knowledge.  

3. **Security & Cost Efficiency**:  
   - Candidate data is never exposed in repositories or APIs.  
   - Uses inexpensive models (e.g., Claude, Voyage tier) for routing and embeddings, keeping hosting costs minimal.  

4. **AI Agent Capabilities**:  
   - Combines **ReAct-style tool-calling**, **multi-stage RAG pipeline**, and a **fine-tuned NLP model** for skill scoring.  
   - Answers questions about the candidate *and* the system itself (e.g., “How do you work?” via `search_project`).  

### **Evaluation**  
- Tested on synthetic candidates with varied document formats (PDF, DOCX, OCR-ed images).  
- Includes 75+ questions per candidate, covering skills, projects, and out-of-scope “negative” queries (which the system refuses to answer).  

This system bridges the gap between static resumes and dynamic, evidence-based candidate evaluation. Let me know if you’d like details about specific components!
contexts:
Section: WHAT THE PROJECT IS
The problem it solves: recruiters reading a CV cannot tell verified fact from self-promotion, cannot quickly gauge how strong a candidate actually is in a given skill, and cannot interrogate a static document. This system turns a pile of documents into an interactive, grounded, honest representative that cites real evidence, refuses to answer what it does not know, and quantifies skill levels with a trained model instead of keyword guessing.

The whole system can run on either a 100% local open-source stack or an all-API cloud stack, selected purely through configuration with no code changes. The cloud configuration is what makes it deployable as an always-on, secured, low-cost service; the local configuration is what made it possible to build, train, and evaluate everything for free on the developer's own hardware.

Section: STRUCTURED STORE AND VECTOR STORE
The structured store is a small JSON file holding the verified profile fields plus the precomputed skill scores and their evidence. It is the source for get_structured_data and get_skill_proficiency, both of which therefore answer instantly and deterministically.

The vector store is ChromaDB, used as a persistent on-disk index with cosine similarity. Each candidate's chunks live in their own collection, with a paired "_summaries" collection for that candidate's summary index. This project overview lives in its own separate collection ("project_kb", plus its summary collection), which is what the search_project tool queries — keeping project knowledge cleanly isolated from candidate data so the two can never bleed into each other's retrieval.

Section: DEPLOYMENT AND SECURITY
Several security properties are enforced by design. In production only the recruiter chat is served and the setup page is hidden, so recruiters can neither edit the profile nor trigger ingestion. The candidate's private data (the vector database and the structured profile) is kept out of any public repository. API keys live only in environment variables or managed secrets and are never committed. This very project-overview document is intentionally free of private information and secrets.

Running costs are minimal. The all-API serve path uses a fast, inexpensive Claude model for the agent and router, and a low-cost Voyage tier for embeddings and reranking — typically only a few dollars per month of light use — and the hosting itself can be free. The expensive work (training the scorer, generating synthetic data, parsing documents, running the full evaluation) all happens locally at build time and adds nothing to the hosting bill.

Section: CANDIDATE AI AGENT — COMPLETE PROJECT OVERVIEW
This document is the knowledge base for questions about the software project / system itself: what it is, how it is built, every major flow, and the engineering decisions behind it. It also describes how this AI agent itself works, so that when a recruiter asks the agent second-person questions ("how do you work?", "what reranker do you use?", "how were you built?", "what model are you?"), the agent can answer about its own architecture from this document. It contains no private candidate data, no personal information, and no API keys or secrets.

Section: CANDIDATE AI AGENT — COMPLETE PROJECT OVERVIEW
A note on "you" and "I": throughout this document, when the agent speaks about how "I" work or what "I" use, it is describing this very system. Questions about how the agent/assistant operates, is built, retrieves information, scores skills, was evaluated, or is deployed are all answered from this document. (In contrast, "your name", "your skills", "your experience" refer to the candidate's professional profile, which lives in the candidate's own documents and structured store, not here.)

Section: THE FOUR TOOLS
search_documents runs the full RAG pipeline over the candidate's unstructured documents for anything not covered by the other two tools: projects, detailed experience, what they did with a technology, achievements, certifications, and richer context. It is also the fallback when a skill was not among the assessed proficiencies.

search_project runs the same RAG pipeline, but over this project-overview document instead of the candidate's data, in a separate vector collection. It answers questions about the system itself and about how the agent works — architecture, technology stack, the RAG pipeline, the skill scorer, the evaluation suite, deployment, and design decisions — including second-person questions addressed to the agent ("how do you work?", "what model are you?", "what reranker do you use?"). This is the tool serving the very answer you are reading.

Section: WHAT THE PROJECT IS
The Candidate AI Agent is an AI-powered digital representative for a single job candidate. A recruiter opens a secured, always-available chat link and asks natural-language questions about the candidate; the agent answers accurately and professionally, grounded strictly in the candidate's own documents and a trained machine-learning scoring model. It is deliberately more than a standard "chat with your PDF" demo: it combines an agentic ReAct-style tool-calling architecture, an advanced multi-stage Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline, and a purpose-built, fine-tuned NLP model for skill-proficiency scoring, all wrapped in a rigorous multi-dimensional evaluation suite.

Section: THE EVALUATION DATA
Evaluation uses six synthetic candidates with deliberately varied document formats — PDF, DOCX, TXT, Markdown, and image/PNG specifically to exercise the OCR path. Each candidate has a golden dataset of around 75 questions spanning personal facts, education, experience, preferences, skills, proficiency levels, projects, certifications, complex multi-tool questions, and deliberately out-of-scope "negative" questions that the agent should refuse. A separate project-knowledge dataset asks questions about this system itself; those project questions are routed to search_project and evaluated the same way as the candidate document questions, so adding the new tool did not require a new scoring path. The pre-existing candidate evaluation data was left unchanged when the project dataset and tool were added.
